segments.text: So you don't need to memorize hundreds or thousands

Because longs like to buy those breakouts.

And you also have short sellers.
Short sellers bet against stocks.
If the stock that they're betting against hits a new high,
they stop out, okay?
That's what responsible short sellers do.
So when you add that key multi-day

all the longs are focused on that breakout.

When a short seller covers their shares,
they're buying back to cover.
So you have two groups of buyers
at this key specific breakout point.
When you have those two groups,
usually you get a rush and you get a quick breakout,

if it drops from six to five, and $5 is a round number,

if all those stop losses at $5 a share get taken out,
it creates a tsunami of automated sell orders.
